label: SES-127 / CNV-029 close-out — resolves PI-108 (universal created/last-edited timestamp visibility across all 24 first-class V2 UI panels). Commits 550e10e (slice A) and 2915393 (slices B/C). Co-implemented with a parallel orchestrator whose ~14 panel commits landed separately on main. No decisions. Attaches to WS-013. Resolves PI-108. Identifiers re-verify at apply time.

session.session_title: Implement and resolve PI-108 — universal created/last-edited timestamp visibility across the V2 desktop UI
session.session_description:
Single Claude Code execution at Doug's terminal. CNV-029. Parent workstream WS-013 (Universal created/last-edited timestamp visibility). Medium: chat (claude_code runner). Opened against PI-108 via kickoff WT-057.

Seed: "whats next" -> selected PI-108.

PI-108 / REQ-002 requires that every first-class V2 entity (24 governance + methodology panels with a browsable desktop UI) surface a formatted created timestamp and, for mutable types, a last-edited timestamp, extending the PI-107 pattern (commit f690bde, the Planning Items panel) to the whole UI.

Delivered across two commits on branch pi-108-finish:
1. Slice A (550e10e): a shared created_updated_section() helper in _governance_helpers.py (formats via the PI-107 datetime_format.format_timestamp helper; updated_field=None renders an em dash for immutable types) plus the four panels that previously showed a raw unformatted updated_at or nothing — Sessions, Workstreams, Conversations (formatted Created + Updated list columns replacing the raw ISO column; created/last-edited detail section) and Commits (a Record-timestamps detail section for the governance commit_created_at/updated_at, kept distinct from the git commit_committed_at).
2. Slices B/C (2915393): the remaining nine panels not yet on main — the immutable/singleton types topics, deposit_events, charter, status (created + em-dash edited) and the methodology panels processes, manual_config, test_spec, crm_candidates, engagements.

Parallel co-implementation: a concurrent orchestrator independently implemented PI-108 for ~14 panels (decisions, risks, reference_books, work_tickets, close_out_payloads, domains, entities, field, requirements, persona) which landed on main (e8d0c19) while this work was in flight, and it was actively switching the working-copy branch between main and pi-111-heartbeat. To avoid clobbering, this session preserved its subagents' work in a git stash, branched pi-108-finish from main once the tree settled, and extracted only the nine complementary panels. It also repaired two defects the parallel work left on main: persona.py imported the helper but never used it (dangling import, no display) — replaced with a complete implementation + matching test; and test_domains/test_entities/test_requirements asserted the pre-Created column sets though their panels had gained the column — updated to match.

Validation: all 24 first-class panels now display timestamps; full 834-test UI suite green; ruff clean. The implementing slices used parallel subagents over disjoint panel batches with a shared, tested helper.

No decisions authored (straightforward replication of an approved pattern; the race handling was operational). Resolves PI-108. The commits sit on pi-108-finish (one commit ahead of main) awaiting Doug's review/merge and push.
session.session_executive_summary: Implemented and resolved PI-108 / REQ-002: universal created/last-edited timestamp visibility across all 24 first-class V2 desktop panels, extending the PI-107 pattern via a shared helper. Slice A (550e10e) added the helper plus the four raw-ISO panels (Sessions/Workstreams/Conversations/Commits); slices B/C (2915393) added the remaining nine (topics, deposit_events, charter, status immutable; processes, manual_config, test_spec, crm_candidates, engagements). A parallel orchestrator co-implemented ~14 panels onto main while racing the branch; this session preserved its work in a stash, branched pi-108-finish off the settled tree, extracted only complementary panels, and repaired the parallel work's broken persona and three stale tests. Full 834-test UI suite green. Resolves PI-108.

conversation.conversation_title: Finishing PI-108 universal timestamp visibility under a parallel-orchestrator race

conversation.conversation_description: Added the shared created_updated_section helper and the timestamp pattern to the 4 raw-ISO panels (slice A) and the 9 remaining immutable/methodology panels (slices B/C), using parallel subagents. Coordinated around a concurrent orchestrator that co-implemented ~14 panels on main and was racing the branch: preserved work in a stash, branched pi-108-finish, extracted only complementary panels, and fixed the parallel work's broken persona + stale tests. 834 UI tests green. Resolves PI-108.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-108 slice A — created/last-edited timestamps on the raw-ISO panels
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-108 slice A — created/last-edited timestamps on the raw-ISO panels

Adds a shared created_updated_section() helper (_governance_helpers) that renders formatted Created / Last Updated rows via the PI-107 format_timestamp helper, em dash for missing (so immutable types pass updated_field=None). Applies the PI-107 pattern to Sessions, Workstreams, Conversations (formatted Created + Updated list columns replacing the raw ISO updated_at; created/last-edited detail section) and Commits (a Record-timestamps detail section for commit_created_at/updated_at, distinct from the git commit_committed_at column). Addresses PI-108 (WS-013); slices B and C follow.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-108 slices B/C — finish universal timestamp visibility (remaining 9 panels)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-108 slices B/C — finish universal timestamp visibility (remaining 9 panels)

Completes PI-108 by adding the created/last-edited timestamp pattern to the 9 panels not yet done on main: the immutable/singleton types (topics, deposit_events, charter, status — created + em-dash edited) and the methodology panels (processes, manual_config, test_spec, crm_candidates, engagements). Also repairs two inconsistencies left in the parallel PI-108 work already on main: persona.py imported created_updated_section but never used it (dangling import) — replaced with a complete implementation + matching test; and test_domains/test_entities/test_requirements asserted the old column sets though their panels had gained the Created column — updated to match. All 24 first-class panels now show created (and, for mutable types, last-edited) timestamps. Full 834-test UI suite green; ruff clean.
